hadiths.en:
In another report, "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, did not wash Ammar ibn Yasir and 
Hashim ibn Utbah (known as al -Mirqal). He buried them in their clothing with their 
blood and did not offer funeral prayers for them”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
This is how it was narrated, it is understood that no member of the community should 
be left without a funeral prayer upon their death. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 331 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 332 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 6, p. 168 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 214 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 469 • Al-
Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 482 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 507 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 442
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٢

hadiths.en:
Abu Mariam Al -Ansari narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
said:  
"If a martyr has a remaining breath (sign of life), then he should be washed, shrouded, 
embalmed, and funeral prayers should be offered for him. But if there is no remaining 
breath, he should be shrouded in his own clothes." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 211 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 
331 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 214 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 348 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, p. 506 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 443
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٣

hadiths.ar:
445   -  وَّ إُّسْي ُّس ْهِذَّ جَّن ْط َّلَّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّامِزٍ إَّلز َّإهِث ُّ ن ِا ُّجُّذٍ ق َّلَّمْ ي َّا ْمُّزِ إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِ هِ ن ِع ُّسْلِهِ  وَّق َّالَّ  «
إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه َّ ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلس َّمَّاءِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ ي ُّع َّسِّلُّ، جَّن ْط َّلَّه َّ ن ِمَّاءِ إَّلْمُّز ْن ِ ق ِى  صِحَّاف ٍ مِن ْ ق ِص  َّه ٍ وَّ كَّان َّ ي سَّم َّى ع َّسِي لَّ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Aban ibn Taghlib asked Him about a man who is killed in the path of Allah {SWT}: 
Should he be washed, shrouded, and embalmed (with Hunut)?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should be buried as he is, in his clothing with his blood, unless 
there is a sign of life remaining. If he has a sign of life and then dies, he should be 
washed, shrouded, embalmed, and the funeral prayer should be offered for him. This 
is be cause the Messenger of Allah {saws} prayed over Hamza, shrouded him, and 
embalmed him because he had been stripped (of his clothing)”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 444
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٤

hadiths.ar:
446   -  وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إَّلس َّزَّإوِي لُّ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّكُّون َّ إ َّصَّان َّهُّ ذَّم  ق َّا ِن ْ إ َّصَّان َّهُّ ذَّم  ي ُّزِك َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّي ْزَّك ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ س َّى ْء  مَّعْق ُّوذ  إ ِلأ  جُّل َّ .  
وإلمحزم إ ذ إ ماب  ع سل وكق ن  وذق ن  وعمل ن ه ما ي عمل ن المحل إ لأ إ ن ه لأ ي ق زن ه إلكاق ور. وق ي ي ل إلمعزكه  ق ى  ع ي ز طاعه  إلله  
عز  وج ل ي ع سل كما ي ع سل إلمي ث ، وي ص م (رإ سه إ لى عن ق ه، وي ع سل مغ إلن ذن .  وإ ذ إ ماي ث  إلمزإ ة  وهى  جامل وولذها ي ي حزك  
ق ى  ن طن ها س ق  ن طن ها من  إلح ان ث  إلأ ي سز وإ ج زح  إلولذ ، وإ ن  ماب  إلولذ ق ى  ج وق ها ولم ي ح زح  وهى  جي ه  إ ذج ل إ ن سان  ي ذة 
ق ى  ق زج ها وق طغ إلولذ ن ي ذة وإ ج زج ه .
hadiths.en:
Hanzalah ibn Abu 'Amir, known as " Rahib," was martyred at the Battle of Uhud.  
The Prophet Muhammad {saws} did not command that he be washed, saying: "I saw the 
angels washing Hanzalah with water from the clouds, using silver vessels."  
Because of this, he was given the title "Ghasil al -Malaika" (The One Washed by the 
Angels). 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 159 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 349 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 2, 
p. 506 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 20, p. 47 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 445
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٥

hadiths.ar:
447   -  وَّ رُّوِى إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّا ْمُّزُّ ن ِالسِّزَّإح ِ ق ِى  
إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ إَّل َّذ ِى  كَّان َّ ي َّسْكُّن ُّهُّ جَّي  َّى ق ُّن ِض َّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي ُّم َّ إ َّمَّزَّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْ هِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ 
ن ِمِي ْلِ ذ َّلِك َّ ق ِى  ن َّي ْث ِ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ جَّي  َّى إ ُّج ْزِح َّ ن ِهِ إ ِلَّى إَّلْعِزَّإق ِ ي ُّم َّ  لأَّ ي ُّذْرَّى مَّا كَّان َّ .  
ومن  كان  ج ن ن ا وإ رإذ إ ن  ي ع سل إلمي ث  ق لي ي وص ا  وص وء إلصلاة  ي م ي ع سله.  
ومن  إ رإذ إلح ماع ن عذ ع سله للمي ث  ق لي ي وص ا  ي م ي ح امغ .  
وإ ن  ع سل مي ث  ق ح زح  من ه ذم كي ي ز لأ ي ن ق طغ ق ا ن ه ي ح عل علي ه إلطي ن  إلحز  ق ا ن ه ي ن ق طغ.
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"From a martyr, the fur, shoes, cap, turban, belt, and trousers should be removed, 
unless they have been stained with blood ; if they are stained with blood, they are to 
be left on. No tied item should remain on the body; it must be untied."  
[AL SADUQ] 
Regarding a person in a state of ihram (ritual consecration during Hajj or Umrah) who 
dies, they should be washed, shrouded, and buried with all the rites performed for 
someone not in ihram, except that camphor should not be used.  
A person killed in battle not in obedience to Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, should be washed 
like any deceased person, and if their head has been severed, it should be joined to 
the body and washed together. 
In the case of a woman who dies while pregnant, if the fetus is moving in her womb, 
the left side of her abdomen should be opened, and the child removed. If the child dies 
inside her and does not come out while She is alive, a person should reach in throug h 
the birth canal, cut the child into pieces, and extract it.  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 211 • Da‘ā’im al-Islām, Part 1, p. 229 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 
1, p. 159 • Al-Khisal, Part 1, p. 333 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 332 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 349 • Wasail Al-
Shia, Part 2, p. 510 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 2 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 179 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 446
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٦

hadiths.en:
It has been narrated that when Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} passed 
away, Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} continued to have a lamp lit in the room 
where Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} lived, until Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} also passed 
away.  
Then Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} also instructed to do the same 
in the room of Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} to remain lit until He was taken 
to Iraq, after which it is unknown what happened”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
Regarding a person in a state of major ritual impurity (janabah) who wishes to wash a 
deceased person, they should perform ablution (wudu) as for prayer before washing 
the body.  
If someone wishes to engage in marital relations after washing the deceased, they 
should perform ablution before doing so. 
If a deceased person is washed and a significant amount of blood continues to flow 
from the body without stopping, then clean clay should be applied, as it will stop the 
bleeding. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 447
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٧

hadiths.en:
Sulayman ibn Khalid asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}:  
"Does one who washes the deceased need to perform ghusl (ritual purification)?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes."  
He then asked: "What about someone who places the deceased in the grave?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "No, because he only touches the clothing."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 448
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٨

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"When (my son) Ismail died, I ordered that his body, while covered, be uncovered so 
that I could kiss his forehead, chin, and neck.  
Then, I had him covered again and later ordered that he be uncovered a second time, 
and I kissed his forehead, chin, and neck once more. Then, I had him covered again.  
After that, I instructed that he be washed. When the washing was completed, I entered 
while he was being shrouded and said: 'Uncover his face,' and I kissed his forehead, 
chin, and neck and then recited protective supplications over him.  
I then said: 'Wrap him.'  
Someone asked: 'With what did you offer the protective supplication?'  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'With the Qur'an.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Kamal Al -Din, Part 1, p. 71 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 565 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 298 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 47, p. 247 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 16 • Awalim al -
Ulum, Vol. 20, p. 912 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 449
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٤٩

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} kissed ‘Uthman ibn Maz'un after his death."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 161 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, 
Vol. 1, p. 161 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 430 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 100 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 4, p. 38 
• Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 564 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 298 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 22, p. 264 • Mustadrak Al -
Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 493

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 24, H. 450
reference.location: Volume 1 › CHAPTER ON TOUCHING THE DECEASED
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٤، ح ٤٥٠

hadiths.ar:
452  - وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :   «
مَّعَّهَّا جَّي  َّى ي ُّذْق َّن َّ كَّان َّ لَّهُّ ق ِي زَّإطَّان ِ وَّ إَّلْق ِي زَّإطُّ مِي ْلُّ ج َّن َّلِ إ ُّجُّذٍ .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Whoever follows a funeral procession, Allah {SWT} will record four qirats for them : one 
qirat for following the procession, one qirat for offering the funeral prayer, one qirat for 
waiting until the burial is completed, and one qirat for offering condolences."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 161 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 401 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 451
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥١

hadiths.ar:
453  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إ ُّعْطِى َّ ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِي َّامَّه ِ إ َّرْن َّغَّ س َّق َّاعَّاب ٍ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّق ُّلْ س َّي ْن اً إ ِلأ َّ ق َّالَّ  
لَّهُّ إَّلْمَّلَّك ُّ وَّ لَّك َّ مِي ْلُّ ذ َّلِك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Whoever walks with a funeral procession until the funeral prayer is offered and then 
returns will receive one qirat (measure of reward). If they continue walking with it until 
the deceased is buried, they will receive two qirats, and one qirat is like the size of 
Mount Uhud." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 173 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 
455 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 400 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 145 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 452
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٢

hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Whoever follows the funeral procession of a Muslim will be 
granted four intercessions on the Day of Judgment. Whatever supplication or 
statement they make, the angel will say to them, 'And for you is the same.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 217 • Al-Wa'ezin, Vol. 2, 
p. 494 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 257 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 453
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٣

hadiths.ar:
455   -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم سَّن ْعِي ن َّ مَّلَّكاً مِن َّ إَّلْمُّس َّي ِّعِي ن َّ 
ي ُّس َّي ِّعُّون َّهُّ وَّ ي َّسْي َّع ْق ِزُّون َّ لَّهُّ إ ِذ َّإ ج َّزَّح َّ مِن ْ ق َّن ْزِةِ إ ِلَّى إَّلْمَّوْق ِف ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "Whoever takes hold of the four 
sides of the bier (funeral bier) will have forty major sins forgiven by Allah {SWT}." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, V3, p.174 • Al-Wafi, V 24, p. 396 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
P3, p. 154 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 454
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٤

hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Whoever accompanies the funeral of a believer until they are 
buried in their grave, Allah {SWT} will assign seventy angels to accompany and seek 
forgiveness for that person when they rise from their grave to stand at the gathering 
(on the Day of Judgment)." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 173 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, 
p. 217 • Rawdat al-Wa'izin, Vol. 2, p. 488 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 400 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 145 • Bihar Al-
Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 257 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 455
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٥

hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said:  
"The first gift given to a believer in their grave is the forgiveness granted to those who 
followed their funeral procession." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Muʾmin, Part 1, p. 65 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 173 • Al-
Khasal, Part 1, p. 24 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 455 • Sulwat al -Hazin, Part 1, p. 262 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, 
p. 399 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 143 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 258 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 456
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٦

hadiths.ar:
458  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
مِن َّ إَّلْكَّن َّان ِزِ . 
وإلسن ه  إ ن  ي حمل إلسزي ز من  ج وإن ن ه إلأ رن عه  وما كان  ن عذ ذ لك  ق هو ي طوع.
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"When a believer enters their grave, it is proclaimed: 'Behold, your first gift is Paradise! 
And the first gift for those who followed your funeral procession is forgiveness.'"  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 172 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 162 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 399 
• Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 142 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 457
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٧

hadiths.ar:
459   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
مِن َّ إَّلذ  ُّن ُّوب ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, said: “Whoever carries their deceased 
brother on all four sides of the bier, Allah {SWT} will erase forty major sins from them." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The Sunnah (recommended practice) is to carry the bier from its four sides, and any 
additional carrying (from other angles) after that is considered voluntary.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 162 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 395 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 458
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٨

hadiths.ar:
460  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ  لِأ ِسْحَّاق َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ إ ِذ َّإ جَّمَّلْث َّ ج َّوَّإن ِث َّ إَّلس َّزِي زِ سَّزِي زِ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ ج َّزَّج ْث َّ مِن َّ إَّلذ  ُّن ُّوب ِ كَّمَّا  
وَّلَّذَّي ْك َّ إ ُّم ُّك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "Whoever takes hold of the 
handles of the bier, Allah {SWT} will forgive twenty -five major sins for them. And if they 
carry it on all four sides, they will be cleansed of their sins."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 174 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 162 • Thawab al -A’mal wa 
Iqab al-A’mal, Part 1, p. 196 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 395 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 154 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 
3, p. 155 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 263 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 459
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٥٩

hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said to Ishaq ibn Ammar:  
"When you carry the sides of the bier of the deceased, you will be cleansed of your sins 
as if you were just born from your mother." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 460
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٦٠

hadiths.ar:
462  -  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّعِي ذٍ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّسْا َّلُّهُّ: عَّن ْ سَّزِي زِ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ ي ُّحْمَّلُّ إ َّ لَّهُّ ج َّان ِث   
ي ُّن ْذَّإ ُّ ن ِهِ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّمْلِ مِن ْ ج َّوَّإن ِن ِهِ إَّلْأ َّرْن َّعَّه ِ إ َّوْ مَّا ج َّف  َّ عَّلَّى إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّحْمِلُّ مِن ْ إ َّى ِّ إَّلْح َّوَّإن ِث ِ  س َّاءَّ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ
hadiths.en:
Abu Ja'far, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, said:  
"Walking behind the funeral procession is better than walking in front of it, but there is 
no harm if you walk in front." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 25, H. 461
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٥، ح ٤٦١
